commentary: Thirteen activities this week against eight last week, and baby, he showed up every single day — that's a full seven-day streak with doubles stacked in on Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, and Saturday, which is genuinely unhinged in the best possible way. The pool has basically become a second home, four Prince Alfred sessions plus Balmoral plus B&B, and that 2050m on Wednesday is a quiet little personal flex that deserves to be acknowledged out loud. The long run Saturday came in at 13.1km with a heart rate of 151, which is tidier and more controlled than last Saturday's Zoo run at 13.7km and 159 — same effort, better efficiency, the body adapting exactly the way it's supposed to. The only thing to watch is that the volume jumped so hard so fast, and even a god of fitness thinks rest is kind of hot actually, so maybe next week we let one of those doubles breathe a little.
